prompt.content:
I'm preparing a report on device upgrade patterns for our finance team, using our historical transaction data which reportedly contains some data recording inconsistencies from the legacy system migration. They want to understand how quickly customers are adopting newly released devices to help with inventory planning.

For all smartphone purchases made in 2024 and 2025, calculate the average number of days between when each device model was released and when customers actually purchased it. Also report the 75th percentile adoption lag to identify when the bulk of early adoption has occurred, and the count of distinct device models that had at least 10 purchases during this period. These metrics will inform us about typical adoption lag from official availability and help forecast future release cycles.

Use the tables device_purchases and devices for this analysis (join on device_id), and round the average and percentile to the nearest whole number of days. Provide your full SQLite query and the final results.

rubrics.name: Reports average number of days between device release and customer purchase as... (ver_3bb1a3596a4e4a2a91b6d98ece736074)

messages.content: Reports average number of days between device release and customer purchase as 709 (strict threshold).

rubrics.name: Reports 75th percentile adoption lag as 896 days (strict threshold) (ver_f21c3e9bd87c4d78badef003e225639c)

messages.content: Reports 75th percentile adoption lag as 896 days (strict threshold).

rubrics.name: Reports count of distinct device models with at least 10 purchases as 33... (ver_9987aa36c4724dd1988a61037b7839b0)

messages.content: Reports count of distinct device models with at least 10 purchases as 33 (strict threshold).

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
avg_adoption_lag_days
709

percentile_75_adoption_lag_days
896

device_models_with_10plus_purchases
33

``` sql 


WITH valid_purchases AS (
    SELECT
        dp.purchase_id,
        dp.device_id,
        dp.purchase_date,
        d.release_date,
        julianday(dp.purchase_date) - julianday(d.release_date) AS adoption_lag_days
    FROM device_purchases dp
    JOIN devices d ON dp.device_id = d.device_id
    WHERE d.device_type = 'smartphone'
      AND strftime('%Y', dp.purchase_date) IN ('2024', '2025')
      AND julianday(dp.purchase_date) >= julianday(d.release_date)
),
device_volume AS (
    SELECT
        device_id,
        COUNT(*) AS purchase_count
    FROM valid_purchases
    GROUP BY device_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10
)
SELECT
    ROUND(AVG(vp.adoption_lag_days)) AS avg_adoption_lag_days,
    (
        SELECT ROUND(adoption_lag_days)
        FROM valid_purchases
        ORDER BY adoption_lag_days
        LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) * 0.75 AS INTEGER) FROM valid_purchases)
    ) AS percentile_75_adoption_lag_days,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM device_volume) AS device_models_with_10plus_purchases
FROM valid_purchases vp;

```
